title: messages: PeerReceipt.receipt_type and PeerGroupUpdate.action are unvalidated free strings
description: ClearMessage::PeerReceipt.receipt_type (core/src/messages.rs line 88) and PeerGroupUpdate.action (line 114) are doc-commented as fixed-value enumerations (delivered|read and join|leave|role_change respectively) but typed as String. Any value passes deserialization. In nie-daemon/src/relay.rs (line 468), receipt_type is passed as a string to update_message_delivery_state(), which writes it to the database. An adversary who can send messages could inject arbitrary strings into the delivery_state column. These fields should be Rust enums with serde tag variants to make invalid values a parse error at the boundary, not a database write.
